selected_plan.title: Define the Uigwe wrapper surface without changing the core protocol
selected_plan.summary: Add the documentation and contract pieces needed for a future wrapper while keeping the current planner and consumer artifacts stable.

retained_alternatives.title: Keep everything inside PROTOCOL.md only
retained_alternatives.summary: Avoid a dedicated wrapper document and extend protocol guidance instead.
cons: Weaker separation between planner and wrapper surface

nodes.title: Add a wrapper-facing surface to the existing Uigwe docs set
nodes.description: Prepare a future wrapper entry point without destabilizing the current Uigwe protocol artifacts.
nodes.why: The next layer above Uigwe needs a stable invocation surface that does not blur the planner core.
acceptance_criteria: The wrapper surface is compatible with existing Uigwe artifacts
constraints: Do not redesign packet or goal-tree contracts
Fit cleanly into the existing docs package
assumptions: A wrapper layer will be implemented later
The current docs package is the right place to define the contract first
risks: Wrapper docs may accidentally duplicate protocol responsibilities

nodes.title: Protect planner versus wrapper boundaries
nodes.description: Make sure wrapper-facing artifacts sit on top of the protocol instead of mutating it.
nodes.why: Boundary clarity is the main brownfield risk in this change.
acceptance_criteria: Planner responsibilities stay inside protocol docs
Wrapper responsibilities stay in wrapper-facing artifacts
constraints: Do not redefine existing packet or goal-tree artifacts
assumptions: A dedicated wrapper document is clearer than protocol-only guidance
risks: Boundary blur between wrapper and protocol surfaces

nodes.title: Integrate the wrapper surface into the existing docs package
nodes.description: Make the wrapper discoverable and well linked inside the current Uigwe document set.
nodes.why: Brownfield additions should fit the current package rather than feel bolted on.
acceptance_criteria: Wrapper docs are discoverable from the existing index
Protocol and wrapper docs cross-link cleanly
assumptions: The docs index is the primary discovery path
alternatives: Leave wrapper docs unlinked until implementation

nodes.title: Write the wrapper surface draft
nodes.description: Document wrapper purpose, entry modes, inputs, outputs, and non-goals.
nodes.why: A future wrapper needs a clear contract before implementation starts.
acceptance_criteria: Planner and wrapper boundaries are explicit
constraints: Do not redefine the planner core
assumptions: A document-first wrapper surface is the right next step
risks: The draft may duplicate protocol responsibilities
done_criteria: Wrapper purpose, inputs, outputs, and non-goals are documented

verification.description: Confirm the wrapper doc does not redefine planner responsibilities.

reentry_triggers: If executor-side todo decomposition cannot produce actionable leaves without changing scope, recommend Uigwe local_reexploration.
If execution invalidates the selected design approach, recommend Uigwe brainstorming.
If success criteria, non-goals, or must-preserve behavior are contradicted, recommend deep_interview or human_review.

nodes.title: Define the wrapper input and output contract
nodes.description: Draft a machine-facing schema for future wrapper requests and wrapper outputs.
nodes.why: The wrapper should pass through Uigwe artifacts without inventing a vague interface.
acceptance_criteria: The contract points at existing Uigwe artifacts
constraints: Do not replace existing packet or goal-tree formats
assumptions: The wrapper will need a machine-facing surface
risks: The schema may accidentally overlap with existing packet contracts
done_criteria: Wrapper input and output fields are explicit

verification.description: Check compatibility with packet and goal-tree contracts.

nodes.title: Integrate the wrapper surface into the Uigwe docs package
nodes.description: Update the Uigwe docs index and cross-links so the wrapper surface is discoverable and properly bounded.
nodes.why: Brownfield additions should fit the existing docs package cleanly.
acceptance_criteria: The docs index links to the wrapper surface
Cross-links do not duplicate protocol definitions
assumptions: Index and protocol cross-links are enough for discoverability
risks: Fragmented navigation if links are incomplete
alternatives: Leave wrapper docs unlinked until implementation
done_criteria: Uigwe docs index links to the wrapper surface
Cross-links point between wrapper and protocol artifacts where needed

verification.description: Confirm the wrapper surface is discoverable without duplicating protocol text.

risk_summary.description: The wrapper spec may accidentally redefine core protocol responsibilities.
risk_summary.mitigation: Add explicit planner vs wrapper boundary language.

risk_summary.description: The new surface may fragment the docs package if linked poorly.
risk_summary.mitigation: Update the docs index and cross-link from protocol surfaces.
